name: TensorOrbit-JARVIS-v2 raw acquisition and full-O(3) target-cache build v2
purpose: Reconstruct a source-pinned TensorOrbit-JARVIS-v2 artifact with the correct distinction between an SO(3) tensor orbit and O(3) crystal point-group compatibility. This does not modify TensorOrbit-JARVIS-v1, any historical Gate report, or any checkpoint.

raw_record_contract.tensor: finite 3x6 piezoelectric matrix; source order and engineering shear are stored per record and canonicalized to [xx,yy,zz,yz,xz,xy]
raw_record_contract.target: Cartesian 3x3x3 polar tensor symmetric in final two indices. It is Reynolds-projected over the complete O(3) crystallographic point group of the source CIF, including mirrors/inversion when present.
raw_record_contract.tensor_orbit: The downstream GaugeFlow condition orbit remains proper SO(3) only. Improper operations are compatibility constraints on a physical polar tensor, not latent alignment gauges.
raw_record_contract.cell_convention: row lattice r=f@L; an integral GL(3,Z) Niggli basis change changes only L/f representation, never the Cartesian tensor directly

activation_inputs.exclusions: a versioned JSON mapping every raw record not in the 4,998 split to an explicit reason
activation_inputs.zero_tensors: retained as physical targets and independently counted; an inversion-compatible polar rank-three target must be exactly zero up to declared numerical tolerance
activation_inputs.joins: unique material_id joins between raw records, split, CSV outputs and hashed target-cache files

promotion_rule: A successful build is provenance activation only. Formula-group leakage, StructureMatcher near-duplicate audit, response strata audit, and two matched external-oracle qualifications remain separate requirements before real-tensor model evaluation.
